channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Rich in cash, Japan automaker Toyota builds a city to test futuristic mobility
item.description: Woven City near Mount Fuji is where Japanese automaker Toyota plans to test everyday living with robotics, artificial intelligence and autonomous zero-emissions transportation.
item.dc:creator: Yuri Kageyama, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Brazil Supreme Court justice orders Rumble suspension nationwide for alleged non-compliance
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — A Brazilian Supreme Court justice on Friday ordered the suspension of video-sharing platform Rumble in the South American nation for allegedly refusing to comply with court orders.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Amazon concedes that Chime SDK makes far more sense than the Chime application itself
item.description: Amazon’s announcement on Wednesday that it is abandoning its Chime collaboration app, while stressing that it will double down on the far more successful Chime software development kit (SDK), was an example of Amazon being Amazon. It knows what it does well, and where to focus.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Trump’s China tariffs will hit small device makers hardest
item.description: The day after the Super Bowl, ZapperBox quietly raised the price on Amazon of its over-the-air DVR. ZapperBox offers one of the best means of recording local channels from an antenna, and had been charging $275 for its flagship model.
item.dc:creator: Jared Newman, Fast Company

item.title: Sweden is investigating a damaged cable in the Baltic Sea
item.description: STOCKHOLM (AP) — Swedish authorities said on Friday they were investigating a damaged cable that was discovered in the Baltic Sea, the latest in a string of recent incidents of ruptured undersea cables that have heightened fears of Russian sabotage and spying in the region.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Apple terminates UK data protection after government overreach
item.description: As of 10 a.m. (ET) today, Apple is no longer offering new users in the UK the chance to enable Advanced Data Protection. Essentially, anyone who now wants to enable the feature to protect their data against criminal or state surveillance or exfiltration will no longer be able to do so.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Q&A: East Bay AI safety expert talks about the future of the technology
item.description: Artificial intelligence safety expert and Redwood Research CEO Buck Shlegeris explains the different philosophies guiding A.I. advancement.
item.dc:creator: Chase Hunter, The Mercury News

item.title: Optimization is a biproduct of Army’s network consolidation
item.description: Leo Garciga, the Army’s CIO, said the Unified Network Optimization initiative is helping the service cut costs and duplicative applications. The post Optimization is a biproduct of Army’s network consolidation first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Leaked Nothing Phone 3a and 3a Pro renders tease a mid-range phone that should have Samsung worried
item.description: Thanks to a leaked render, we could have our first proper look at the Nothing Phone 3a and 3a Pro – and even by looks alone, they seem to be worthy challengers for Samsung’s mid-range Galaxy handsets.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: Optoma’s tiny, portable ultra short throw laser projector can throw a 100-inch image from just 25cm away, wherever you take it
item.description: Optoma’s latest ultra short throw projector, the Photon Go, crams a whole lot of technology into a very small (and portable) package.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Rivian records first gross profit as EV policy risks loom
item.description: The California-based company's fourth quarter result easily topped the $32 million expected by Wall Street.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: North Korean hackers are posing as software development recruiters to target freelancers
item.description: Freelance software developers are being hit with infostealing malware to fund the North Korean regime, experts warn.
item.dc:creator: benedict.collins@futurenet.com (Benedict Collins), TechRadar

item.title: Why enterprises fail at finops
item.description: As cloud spending continues to climb, the discipline known as finops (financial operations) has emerged as a critical strategy for managing and optimizing cloud investments. This is no longer news. Finops has been a discipline and a toolkit for years.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Telefónica Tech and Eurofirms People First Join Forces to Revolutionize HR with AI
item.description: Eurofirms People First and Telefónica Tech have partnered to apply artificial intelligence (AI) generative processes to employee selection and human resource management. The collaboration aims to enhance efficiency, productivity, and operational agility by leveraging AI-powered recommendation systems.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Climate change is shrinking glaciers faster than ever, with 7 trillion tons lost since 2000
item.description: A new and massive study finds that climate change is melting the world’s mountain glaciers faster than ever.
item.dc:creator: Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Chaos ahead: emerging technologies will test data privacy, says Britain’s ICO
item.description: Emerging technologies such as connected transport, quantum sensing, healthcare diagnostics, and synthetic AI-driven content are creating huge gaps in data protection and privacy that could quickly outpace the ability of regulators and enterprises to contain them.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Charred EV batteries from California wildfires spark concern about toxic waste contaminating drinking water
item.description: A water agency in charge of ground water for 2 million L.A. County residents has several concerns about hazardous waste processing from the Eaton fire.
item.dc:creator: Steve Scauzillo, The Mercury News

item.title: The Future of Aerospace: True Anomaly Inc. Expands into Southern California
item.description: True Anomaly Inc., a defense technology company, expands its operations to Long Beach, California, with a 90,000-square-foot factory at Douglas Park.
item.dc:creator: Christina Merino, The Mercury News

item.title: Google’s new AI co-scientist aims to speed up the scientific discovery process
item.description: Google has unveiled an AI co-scientist built on its Gemini 2.0 platform, aiming to accelerate scientific research by generating novel hypotheses and refining experiments. While the system shows promise, questions remain about its performance and broader applicability.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft’s new AI hub highlights apps that support NPUs
item.description: Applications that support the specialized AI chips on Copilot+ PCs have been rare, but Microsoft is looking to change that.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Apple unveils a souped-up and more expensive version of its lowest priced iPhone
item.description: Apple has released a sleeker and more expensive version of its lowest priced iPhone in an attempt to widen the audience for a bundle of artificial intelligence technology that the company has been hoping will revive demand for its most profitable product lineup.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 5 ways to fix OneDrive’s worst annoyances on Windows
item.description: On Windows, OneDrive is built right into the operating system. Even if you don’t turn it on, it will pop up occasionally, asking you to “back up your files to the cloud.” What that really means, of course, is that Microsoft wants you to move your folders to OneDrive. I’m not here to knock OneDrive.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Experts give up hope for 157 false killer whales stranded on a beach in Australia's Tasmania state
item.description: Marine experts have given up hope of rescuing more than 150 false killer whales that stranded on a remote beach on Australia’s island state of Tasmania.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Want to transform networking? Empower the missing users
item.description: As 2025 kicked off, I wrote a column about the network vendor landscape – specifically, which networking players will step up and put their efforts into finding new applications with solid business benefits that could enable a network transformation.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: 3 reasons to consider a data security posture management platform
item.description: A week rarely goes by without a major data security breach. Recent news includes a breach impacting an energy company’s 8 million customers, another compromising the information on 450,000 current and former students, and one more exposing 240,000 credit union members.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: A top Chinese official tours Thai-Myanmar border to highlight crackdown on scam centers
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Efforts to shut down online scam centers in Myanmar appeared to gain momentum on Monday as a top Chinese security official visited both sides of the Thai-Myanmar border ahead of expected large-scale repatriations of workers in the illicit industry.
item.dc:creator: JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How to set up a home network like a pro
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson helps you ensure a smooth online experience with a reliable, secure home network setup.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What changes to the CHIPS act could mean for AI growth and consumers
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Even as he's vowed to push the United States ahead in artificial intelligence research, President Donald Trump's threats to alter federal government contracts with chipmakers and slap new tariffs on the semiconductor industry may put new speed bumps in front of the tech industry.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KRON4
